

---
title: "Theorem Environment Test"
format:
    html:
        toc: true
    typst: default
---

## Numbered environments

::: {#thm-test}
## Pythagoras
For a right triangle with legs $a, b$ and hypotenuse $c$,
$$ a^2 + b^2 = c^2. $$

::: {#def-test}
## Group
A *group* is a set $G$ with a binary operation $\cdot: G \times G \to G$ satisfying associativity, identity, and inverse axioms.
:::
:::

::: {#lem-test}
## Yoneda
For a locally small category $\mathcal{C}$ and a functor $F: \mathcal{C}^{\mathrm{op}} \to \mathbf{Set}$,
$$ \mathrm{Nat}(h^A, F) \cong F(A). $$
:::

::: {#prp-test}
## Fact as Proposition
The completed algebraic closure $\widehat{\overline{K}}$ is algebraically closed.
:::

::: {#cor-test}
## 
If $V$ is $B_{\mathrm{cris}}$-admissible, then $V$ is $B_{\mathrm{dR}}$-admissible.
:::

::: {#exm-test}
## Tate module
For an elliptic curve $E$ over $\mathbf{Q}$, the rational Tate module $V_\ell(E)$ is an $\ell$-adic representation of $\mathrm{Gal}_{\mathbf{Q}}$.
:::

::: {#exr-test}
## 
Prove that the specialization map is Galois equivariant.
:::

## Unnumbered environments

::: {.proof}
By direct computation, $(a+b)^2 = a^2 + 2ab + b^2$.
:::

::: {.remark}
This also works in characteristic $\neq 2$.
:::

::: {.solution}
Left as an exercise to the reader.
:::

## Cross-reference test

See @thm-test, @def-test, @lem-test, @prp-test, @cor-test, @exm-test, and @exr-test.
